ucs.title: [LLM] Elderwood / Beijing Group watering-hole 0-day: browser spawns LOLBin and drops PE within 2 minutes (Operation Aurora pattern)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Elderwood (a.k.a. Beijing Group / Sneaky Panda) tradecraft — strategic web compromise (T1189) of industry-relevant sites delivering an IE/Flash/Chromium 0-day (T1203) that breaks out of the renderer, spawns a script host or LOLBin, and stages a packed implant (Hydraq/9002 RAT/Briba/Linfo) on disk via T1105. The exact TTP chain that made the 2009 Google/Aurora intrusion famous and that the group has reused against defense suppliers, NGOs and human-rights targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as spawn_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("iexplore.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","plugin-container.exe","FlashPlayerPlugin*.exe","flashplayerplugin.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.dest
| rename Processes.* as *
| join type=inner host [
    | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as drop_time values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped_file values(Filesystem.file_path) as drop_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as drop_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dat") by host Filesystem.process_name
    | rename Filesystem.process_name as dropping_process
]
| eval delta_sec = (drop_time - spawn_time)
| where delta_sec >= 0 AND delta_sec <= 120
| table spawn_time, drop_time, delta_sec, host, user, parent_process_name, process_name, process, dropped_file, drop_path, drop_hash, dropping_process
| sort - spawn_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 120;
let Browsers = dynamic(["iexplore.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","plugin-container.exe","flashplayerplugin.exe","FlashPlayerPlugin_32_0_0_465.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
// Step 1 - browser-renderer breakout (Aurora-style 0-day exploit chain)
let ExploitSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Browsers)
       or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\Internet Explorer\", @"\Adobe\Flash")
    | where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Browser = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              BrowserCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Child = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ChildSHA256 = SHA256;
// Step 2 - same host drops a fresh PE in a user-writable location (T1105 stage)
ExploitSpawn
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
         or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".dat"
         or FileName endswith ".tmp"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              DroppedFile = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath, DropSHA256 = SHA256,
              DroppingProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceId
| where DropTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project SpawnTime, DropTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', DropTime, SpawnTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, Browser, Child, ChildCmd,
          DroppedFile, DropPath, DropSHA256, DroppingProcess
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 120;
let Browsers = dynamic(["iexplore.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","plugin-container.exe","flashplayerplugin.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
let ExploitSpawn = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (Browsers)
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ParentProcessName, TargetProcessName,
              TargetProcessCommandLine;
ExploitSpawn
| join kind=inner (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\")
    | where TargetFileExtension in~ ("exe","dll","scr","dat","tmp")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
              ActingProcessName
  ) on DvcHostname
| where DropTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project SpawnTime, DropTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', DropTime, SpawnTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ParentProcessName, TargetProcessName,
          TargetProcessCommandLine, TargetFileName, TargetFilePath,
          TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: Elderwood's defining tradecraft is a stockpile of browser/Flash 0-days delivered via strategic web compromise to seed implants like Hydraq, Briba, Linfo and 9002 RAT — exactly what fired in Operation Aurora. Generic browser-spawns-shell rules see plenty of noise from update agents and admin scripts; pairing the renderer-breakout with a co-located PE drop in a user-writable directory inside 120s co

ucs.title: [LLM] Elderwood spearphishing → Office macro → encoded/packed payload staging (T1566 + T1204.002 + T1027.013)
ucs.description: Detects the second Elderwood delivery vector — targeted spearphishing (T1566.001/T1566.002) at defense-supply-chain and NGO recipients carrying weaponised Office docs that, on user execution (T1204.002), spawn a script host whose command line carries the group's hallmark encoded/encrypted staging primitives (base64 PowerShell, certutil -decode of a packed blob, regsvr32 squiblydoo, bitsadmin transfer) consistent with T1027.002/T1027.013.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordpad.exe","mspub.exe","visio.exe","eqnedt32.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process
| rename Processes.* as *
| eval cmd_lc = lower(cmdline)
| where match(cmd_lc, "-encodedcommand|-enc\s|-ec\s|frombase64string|downloadstring|downloadfile|iex |invoke-expression|certutil\s+(-|/)decode|certutil\s+(-|/)urlcache|bitsadmin\s+/transfer|regsvr32\s+/s\s+/n\s+/u\s+/i:|-w(in)?(dow)?style?\s+h(idden)?|scrobj\.dll")
| table first_seen, last_seen, host, user, parent_process_name, process_name, cmdline, child_hash, count
| sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordpad.exe","mspub.exe","visio.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let SuspChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeApps)
| where FileName in~ (SuspChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
// Elderwood-flavoured staging primitives - encoded/encrypted/packed payload pulls
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
        "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-EC ",
        "FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile",
        "IEX ","Invoke-Expression",
        "certutil -decode","certutil -urlcache","certutil /decode","certutil /urlcache",
        "bitsadmin /transfer",
        "regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:","scrobj.dll")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-W(in)?(dow)?Style?\s+H(idden)?"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeApp = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          OfficeCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let OfficeApps = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordpad.exe","mspub.exe","visio.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let SuspChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentBin in~ (OfficeApps)
| where ChildBin in~ (SuspChildren)
| where CommandLine has_any (
        "-EncodedCommand","-enc ","-EC ",
        "FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile",
        "IEX ","Invoke-Expression",
        "certutil -decode","certutil -urlcache","certutil /decode","certutil /urlcache",
        "bitsadmin /transfer",
        "regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:","scrobj.dll")
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)-W(in)?(dow)?Style?\s+H(idden)?"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentBin, ChildBin, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Elderwood-style Office spawn of LOLBin with encoded/packed payload staging
id: 9b2a7d88-3f3a-4e41-9f4f-aurora-elderwood-001
status: experimental
description: Detects Elderwood / Beijing Group spearphishing follow-on - Office app spawns a script host or LOLBin whose command line carries encoded/encrypted payload staging primitives (T1027.013, T1105) consistent with Hydraq/9002 RAT delivery chains.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0066/
  - https://www.symantec.com/content/dam/symantec/docs/security-center/white-papers/elderwood-project-12-en.pdf
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1027.013
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.g0066
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\mspub.exe'
      - '\visio.exe'
      - '\wordpad.exe'
      - '\eqnedt32.exe'
  child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  staging:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '-EncodedCommand'
      - '-enc '
      - '-EC '
      - 'FromBase64String'
      - 'DownloadString'
      - 'DownloadFile'
      - 'IEX '
      - 'Invoke-Expression'
      - 'certutil -decode'
      - 'certutil -urlcache'
      - 'certutil /decode'
      - 'certutil /urlcache'
      - 'bitsadmin /transfer'
      - 'regsvr32 /s /n /u /i:'
      - 'scrobj.dll'
  hidden_window:
    CommandLine|re|i: '-W(in)?(dow)?Style?\s+H(idden)?'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: parent and child and (staging or hidden_window) and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Computer
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate VBA-driven automation that calls PowerShell/cmd with encoded command lines (rare in user-class workstations)
  - Admin macros invoking certutil for legitimate cert decoding
level: high
ucs.rationale: Elderwood is documented (Symantec 'The Elderwood Project', Sept 2012) running parallel campaigns where 0-day-laden Office attachments are spearphished into defense-industrial-base and NGO targets with packed/encoded second-stage loaders for Hydraq/Briba/9002 RAT. Office-spawning-LOLBin alone is too noisy; pairing it with the obfuscated-payload-staging primitives (T1027.013) the group consistently
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
